choice: No. There is no separate retirement-date concept. Each person's salary is an income stream with its own end date; that end date IS the boundary. When the stream ends, the withdrawal order covers the resulting shortfall. Mixed-phase households and SSA claiming age are handled by per-stream start/end dates, not a retirement flag.
rationale: The human ruled that retirement is implicit in the income stream — salary ending and withdrawals filling the gap fully captures the income-side boundary without a dedicated date field. (human-settled during grooming, within discussion of "Does each working Person carry an explicit work-income-end (retirement) date that bounds salary, deferrals, and contributions — i.e., the per-person accumulation→decumulation boundary at which earned income stops and the withdrawal engine begins covering that person's share of the shortfall?")
